content.problemStatement:
For a given pair of arrays AAA and BBB, both of size NNN, we define:


f(A,B)f(A, B)f(A,B) is defined as the minimum cost of a sequence of operations that transforms the array AAA into the array BBB using the following operations:

Rearrange the array AAA arbitrarily for 000 cost.
Choose some index iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), modify AiA_iAi​ to (Ai+1) mod N(A_i + 1) \bmod N(Ai​+1)modN for a cost of 111.


For example, for N=3N = 3N=3, f([0,1,2],[0,0,1])f([0, 1, 2], [0, 0, 1])f([0,1,2],[0,0,1]) is 111 because we can first choose index 333 in AAA to get [0,1,0][0, 1, 0][0,1,0] for cost of 111, and then rearrange to [0,0,1][0, 0, 1][0,0,1] for no cost.

Given an integer NNN, there are N2NN^{2N}N2N pairs of arrays AAA and BBB satisfying the following conditions:


∣A∣=∣B∣=N|A| = |B| = N∣A∣=∣B∣=N
0≤Ai,Bi<N0 \le A_i, B_i < N0≤Ai​,Bi​<N
Find the sum of f(A,B)f(A, B)f(A,B) over all such pairs of arrays modulo PPP, where PPP is a prime number.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and PPP.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of f(A,B)f(A, B)f(A,B) modulo PPP.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 353535.
